name: Semur Banka (Beef in Soy Sauce)
recipeIngredient: 1 ginger piece, peeled and finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the tamarind pulp in a bowl and pour boiling water over it. Set aside until cooled.
2. Strain the mixture through a fine mesh sieve into another bowl, pressing to extract as much liquid as possible. Discard solids.
3. Cut steak into 1-inch pieces.
4. In a separate bowl, mix brown sugar, molasses, and dark soy sauce.
5. Heat oil in a wok until hot. Add onions and stir-fry until softened.
6. Add garlic, ginger, nutmeg, and black pepper; cook for 3 minutes, stirring frequently.
7. Add steak to the wok and increase heat to moderately high. Cook, turning occasionally, until browned on all sides.
8. Stir in remaining ingredients, including reserved tamarind liquid. Bring mixture to a boil.
9. Reduce heat to low, cover pan, and simmer for 2 to 2-1/2 hours or until meat is cooked through and tender; sauce thickens as it simmers.
10. Remove from heat, transfer contents to a warmed serving platter, and serve immediately.
